what_it_does: A standalone HTML preview card demonstrating the screen-title (dashboard H1) pattern: display-700 type at ~36px in bronze, shown via two example titles ('Brand Kit', 'Virtual Hub Settings') each with a descriptive subtitle on a canvas stage.
observations: Carries a @dsCard annotation: group='Brand', name='Screen-title treatment', subtitle='Display 700 in bronze — the dashboard H1 pattern', viewport='700x220'.
Lead text states the rule explicitly: titles are display 700 at ~36px in bronze (#988058) — 'not gold' — used as the H1 on every dashboard surface.
Two live examples styled with .title at 38px/700 bronze plus subtitles describing each screen's purpose.
Example titles/subtitles reference real product surfaces: Brand Kit (visual identity inheritance) and Virtual Hub Settings (menu/navigation/hub variants).
standouts: Explicitly distinguishes bronze (#988058) from gold for titles — a stated do-not-confuse rule.
Names the pattern as the universal dashboard H1 and grounds it in real screens (Brand Kit, Virtual Hub Settings).
conventions: Follows the @dsCard gallery convention and var()-token discipline (canvas bg, radius, fonts, bronze accent).
Lead text hardcodes the literal hex #988058 alongside the --accent-bronze token usage — documenting the value inline.
Title sizes are inline literals as specimen values.
notes: The dashboard screen-title (H1) pattern card; display 700 ~36px in bronze (not gold), demonstrated on two real product screen titles.
questions: Is #988058 the canonical value behind --accent-bronze, kept in sync with the token registry?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard names it the screen-title treatment and that bronze display-700 H1 pattern is what renders.
intention_signals: Intended to fix the dashboard H1 convention by eye and rule — bronze display 700 at ~36px — and prevent confusion with the gold accent.
novelty: The bronze-not-gold title rule made explicit and demonstrated on actual screen titles, distinguishing title colour from accent colour.
contribution: Contributes the dashboard screen-title (H1) treatment — bronze display 700 ~36px — with the explicit bronze-vs-gold rule and real-screen examples.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML @dsCard preview defining the dashboard screen-title H1 pattern as bronze (#988058) display-700 at ~36px, shown on two real product screen titles.
intention_for_embedding: To fix the dashboard H1 convention by eye and rule — bronze display 700, not gold — and ground it in actual screens.
